crime.value: Multiple mysterious crimes including a death (person fell down clothes chute), property intrusion (hole dug in trunk-room wall overnight), and disappearance of character Halsey. Central questions: Who created the hole? Who fell down the chute? What supernatural or criminal agency is at work?
crime.quote: Who was it fell down the clothes chute? Who was it scared Miss Louise almost into her grave?

victim.value: Multiple potential victims: unknown person who fell down clothes chute; Louise (frightened); Halsey (missing). Primary victim unclear from excerpt.
victim.quote: Who was it fell down the clothes chute? Who was it scared Miss Louise almost into her grave?

closed_world.value: A rented country house for the summer occupied by the narrator (middle-aged spinster), family members Halsey and Gertrude, servant Liddy, and household staff. The house becomes a sealed investigative space where crimes occur internally.
closed_world.quote: took a furnished house for the summer out of town, and found herself involved in one of those mysterious crimes

investigator.value: Rachel Innes, the middle-aged spinster narrator who rents the house and undertakes investigation of the crimes. Acts as amateur detective examining evidence (the hole, the trunk-room, physical details).
investigator.quote: I'm doing my best to find out. What in the world are you driving at?
